Dialogues/3: Why have you been gone so long?
Dialogues/4: Your diving suit is getting rusty!
Dialogues/5: We haven't played games together in ages.
Dialogues/6: I've been training alone—this time, you're no match for me!
Dialogues/7: I had the octopus search the entire town.
Dialogues/8: I was worried someone might bully you, boo!
Dialogues/12: This is an old board, full of scratches...
Dialogues/13: They must have used it for a long time!
Dialogues/15: No record of this item in the product database—looks like it's handmade.
Dialogues/16: It must hold some very special meaning!
Dialogues/18: Such an old lamp, feels like it's been used forever.
Dialogues/19: Those three bulbs must be able to light up the darkness!
Dialogues/21: How strange... What kind of person would bring a recycling bin when moving?
Dialogues/22: It seems like it can only be installed underground. Let's help set it up.
Dialogues/23: Thanks to that article, I now have a new office.
Dialogues/24: I'll need your help again to move all my things in.
Dialogues/28: Forget it. Just like before, none of you will believe me anyway.
Dialogues/31: I remember you… You're the newcomer…
Dialogues/32: Before you, no one had come here for a long time.
Dialogues/33: After your arrival, the numbers on the museum started to change.
Dialogues/34: Now you show up again at the scene I'm investigating…
Dialogues/38: Hello? Mr. Sage? My partner seems to have confirmed your theory—
Dialogues/40: <shock a=0.5>Hold on! I'll be there right away!
Dialogues/41: Beep— What a strange person you are!
Dialogues/52: This museum… it's giving off a strange aura.
Dialogues/54: All the answers lie within this building.
Dialogues/55: I don't know why, but I think it's calling out to you.
Dialogues/56: You have the same presence as this museum.
Dialogues/58: Help me uncover the ultimate truth.
Dialogues/65: Even if I told others, they wouldn't believe me.
Dialogues/66: I've always lacked confidence in my discoveries.
Dialogues/67: Like everything I found was a delusion just because nobody believes in me…
Dialogues/68: But you proved to me that it wasn't.
Dialogues/69: Alright, I feel filled with the drive to collect strange patterns—I should keep searching for special beings like you.
Dialogues/70: Maybe there's an even greater truth waiting to be discovered.
Dialogues/82: Have you noticed my little daily whisper column?
Dialogues/83: Have you tried following the rules I put there?
Dialogues/85: If you behave like me, the townsfolk will think you're crazy.
Dialogues/86: By the way, I'm planning to set up a break room in this newspaper office.
Dialogues/87: When the neighbors come by to play Mahjong and chat, that's when I get my intel.
Dialogues/88: The game's about to start, but the room is not ready to receive guests yet.
Dialogues/89: I'm heading out to investigate something, so I'm leaving this place to you!
Dialogues/91: Oh, and there are some boxes of treasures I collected from the far corners of the world that I haven't had time to sort.
Dialogues/92: Please put them on the display shelf in the office!
Dialogues/110: What happened to me just now? I have no memory at all.
Dialogues/120: It feels so comfortable just standing here.
Dialogues/132: Help the client clean up, and they'll surely notice your care!
Dialogues/133: If you can't find it, click ? next to the task.
Dialogues/134: My AI can give you some tips for the task!
Dialogues/151: Hey, <PlayerName>, did you just find an empty house?
Dialogues/152: I heard someone used to live there, but strangely, nobody can find the owner anymore.
Dialogues/153: Even stranger—people don't even remember who lived there at all…
Dialogues/154: Too bad, the trash inside has already started to smell, and it's really hurting the town's image.
Dialogues/155: Luckily, we've got a housekeeping expert like you! Would you mind cleaning it up?
Dialogues/162: Wow, this house looks so beautiful once it's cleaned!
Dialogues/164: …But honestly, leaving it here to rot would be such a waste.
Dialogues/165: I guess, it should be handed over to the right person.
Dialogues/166: <PlayerName> I can't shake the feeling this house has been waiting just for you.
Dialogues/168: I'll hand it over to you. I know it'll be in good hands.
Dialogues/169: Oh, and the developer of this house also offers a floor plan renovation service.
Dialogues/170: If you're not satisfied with the current layout, you can click Change Layout to purchase a remodel.
Dialogues/181: Hey, <PlayerName>, did you just find another empty house?
Dialogues/182: Looks like it's the same situation as last time—I'll need your help to clean it again.
Dialogues/183: With your help, the town is getting cleaner and tidier.
Dialogues/193: the house looks brand new again!
Dialogues/194: If you'd like, please accept this house as well.
Dialogues/195: Just like the last one, it also comes with floor plan renovation services.
Dialogues/196: In your hands, I'm sure it will turn into a cozy home.
Dialogues/203: Even I didn't know this house existed!
Dialogues/204: You already know more about this town than I do, and I'm the Mayor!
Dialogues/205: Same as before—clean it up well, and then keep it safe.
Dialogues/206: I wonder if this house also has renovation services…
Dialogues/207: Never mind. You're just not cut out for this.
Dialogues/208: Just look at your dorky face.
Dialogues/209: Oh, forgot to mention. I'm Dexter, the journalist in town, the smartest person around, and an expert at uncovering the truth.
Dialogues/210: You must have read my newspaper by now. Check your mailbox more often if you haven't.
Dialogues/211: There are so many intriguing things to be reported in this town. My news materials will never run dry.
Dialogues/212: Wait till you see my next amazing piece!
Dialogues/300: Welcome——! Fresh furniture, toasty furniture, come get yours! ヽ( ﾟ▽ﾟ)ノ
Dialogues/301:
Beep beep beep! Good morning, <PlayerName>! (ノ•̀ω•́)ノ
I'm Mark, Cargo Robot No. 4411.
Dialogues/302: From now on, I'll help you move your stuff. Now try taking an item out of my box. （Left Click）
Dialogues/303: See the excitement on my face? Let's get pumped, buddy! σ ` ∀ ´) σ
Dialogues/304: Beep beep! You can place items in any room you like. Try carrying them outside!
Dialogues/306: I've got a lot of stuff (blushing).
Dialogues/307: I'm heading out to buy some daily necessities. Once you're done, feel free to go—no need to wait for me.
Dialogues/309: See that long stretch of wall in the upstairs hallway?
Dialogues/310: That's why I rented this place—perfect for a photo display! (★ ω ★)
Dialogues/311: Please help me hang them all up!
Dialogues/314: She's been extra sleepy since we moved in... no clue why.
Dialogues/315: But don't worry, she's super gentle. Totally not a biter!
Dialogues/317:
"Oooh—ooh ooh ooh, the game console!
(★ ω ★) Put it in my bedroom, okay?"
Dialogues/318: I'm calling my bestie over for an all-night gaming party to celebrate moving in! (p≧w≦q)
Dialogues/340: Sorry, I just noticed you were here.
Dialogues/341: This is Ian's workshop. He was a brilliant alchemist.
Dialogues/342: I remember him standing at the door, excitedly telling me he was going to activate a grand magic circle.
Dialogues/343: After that, he never came out of the workshop again.
Dialogues/344: All these years, I've kept the house just as it was, always thinking he'd return.
Dialogues/345: But now, I think... it's time to let go.
Dialogues/346: I'm going to seal this place for good. Before I leave, please help me clean it one last time.
Dialogues/347: After that, please take good care of this house.
Dialogues/350: You're here! Thanks again for helping me clean up the cabin last time.
Dialogues/351: Returning to my childhood home, I feel a little lighter...
Dialogues/352: I painted the walls again these past few days—hoping it would help me feel better.
Dialogues/353: But that bathtub… yeah, it's kind of weird.
Dialogues/354: There seems to be a bubbling sound underneath.
Dialogues/355: When I was bathing, I even saw an octopus waving at me.
Dialogues/356: (Hmph, youngsters these days... barging into my workshop without even knocking!)
Dialogues/357: No entry to the workshop. Please stay outside.
Dialogues/360: Thanks again for helping me move my things.
Dialogues/361: Now that I've finally made it back to town, I think I'll just stay here in this little cabin and rest for a while…
Dialogues/362: There are 4 brilliant gems missing. They are my most treasured possessions... Sigh.
Dialogues/363: Good thing I have started unpacking. Some of my jewelries are still in the boxes in the basement. (Tired)
Dialogues/364: The rest is your time to shine.
Dialogues/365: Wow, I found a gem here. Why didn't I see it earlier when I was unpacking. (Unbelievable)
Dialogues/366: Gosh, how did it get in there!
Dialogues/367: It was this gem that clogged the toilet these past few days! Hmm...they definitely need a good cleaning.
Dialogues/369: The gems were packed in these metal boxes. Let's open them upstairs.
Dialogues/373: Have you arrived already? Say hello to Mom for me.
Dialogues/374: I brought the picture of the wheat fields with me this time.
Dialogues/375: I hope she will feel better looking at familiar views.
Dialogues/381: Wait... The table is clean and the tiles are smooth...
Dialogues/382: <shock a=0.5>Did I come into the wrong house?
Dialogues/383: My memory is getting worse. Now where's home?
Dialogues/384: Did I clean yesterday...no...hmm...the day before?
Dialogues/386: But staying clean and tidy is still in my instincts.
Dialogues/387: Alright, stranger. I'm going to have to kick you out if you stay longer.
Dialogues/400: Hmm... if it were just a simple theft, things wouldn't be this messy.
Dialogues/401: These marks look more like signs of a struggle...
Dialogues/402: There must be more secrets behind this case waiting to be uncovered.
Dialogues/404: You always seem to be around when strange things happen.
Dialogues/405: Maybe I should write you into my "Whisper Daily"!
Dialogues/406: Alright, the investigation here is done. Time to move on.
Dialogues/407: I have a feeling we'll run into each other again.
Dialogues/1201: There are some broken tombstones in the yard. You can use nearby materials to fix them.
Dialogues/1202: Everything needed for the festival is ready. They're all stored in the wooden crate in the yard.
Dialogues/1203: I'll be in my room if you need me...
Dialogues/1210: Whew, I have way too many books. Thanks for helping me carry them up.
Dialogues/1211: These are all my precious food for the soul ( ^ ω ^).
Dialogues/1212: Please help me place them nicely on the bookshelf.
Dialogues/1220: Hi, welcome to our 'little universe'... Oh! You're here.
Dialogues/1221: Did you bring all the goods?
Dialogues/1222: Perfect! We can open the store today!
Dialogues/1223: This drink is a truly all-natural energy boost—you'll feel like "life is so beautiful"!
Dialogues/1224: If you're starting a fresh new morning, this bread is definitely your "magical key"!
Dialogues/1225: Hic... I want to buy... hic, seaweed chips, five bags... hic…
Dialogues/1227: Could you come back later? We're not open yet.
Dialogues/1232: Tasty Diner is about to open, but there's still a lot to do!
Dialogues/1233: The boxes are full of utensils and seasonings—please help set them on the tables.
Dialogues/1234: I need to go to the river to gather ingredients, so I'll leave the diner to you!
Dialogues/1236: By the way, did you check the kitchen?
Dialogues/1237: Can you also unpack the pots and bowls from the box and set them up—
Dialogues/1239: Oh no! That's a huge fish! …Is it wearing clothes!?
Dialogues/1300: Wow, what a tiny backpack! Try hanging it on the wall—it might look cute there.
Dialogues/1561: Did you find the bus card? With that, you'll be able to use the bus stops outside more easily.
Dialogues/1562: I can't recall exactly when or what it was, but I still wanted to say thank you.
Dialogues/1563: This phonograph was built based on one of Grandpa's old designs, but… well, both of us are a little hard of hearing now, so we don't really have much use for it.
Dialogues/1564: I'm sending it to you—along with one of our treasured records.
Dialogues/1800: The new electric meter is installed. Please don't adjust it yourself.
Dialogues/1801: It's very dangerous because you might risk electrocuting yourself.
Dialogues/1803: Of course! Anything to help a neighbor out.
Dialogues/1804: Are you really okay? Your injury looks bad.
Dialogues/1805: Oh yeah, it's fine. Injuries are normal for my job.
Dialogues/1806: I need to go now. Gotta go set up more meters!
Dialogues/2002: Luna: Did you get it? I hope you like it!
Dialogues/2003: Luna: There's also a vinyl record, but I don't have a record player. It's yours!
Dialogues/2005: Today I was reviewing the logs from the first-generation Mark, and surprisingly, it shows that you once helped him.
Dialogues/2006: If the empty gift boxes are taking up space, you can open Storage and put the boxes away.
Dialogues/2007: The Recycle Bin is on the wall.
Dialogues/2008: Right Click to open the Recycle Bin and drop in items to exchange them for Vouchers.
Dialogues/2009: Recycling value is low, but it's still a way to turn unused items intoVouchers.
Dialogues/2010: Welcome! One furniture surprise bag costs just 10Vouchers—a total bargain with a dash of surprise!
Dialogues/2011: Join the 10x draw now—guaranteed to get a new item you don't already own. Don't miss it!
Dialogues/2012: Each surprise bag contains one hand-picked home item. Try your luck and fill your space with comfort and charm!
Dialogues/2013: We also offer home delivery for furniture—everything goes straight to yourStorage!
Dialogues/2014: Hey, you're back again! You know the drill—grab a surprise bag. I've got a good feeling about your luck today!
Dialogues/2015: After shopping, don't forget to check your storage at home.
Dialogues/2051: It looks so calm and peaceful... wonder who you'll meet here?
Dialogues/2052: Before you start working, let's tidy up your own home.
Dialogues/2053: A clean and comfy home is the key to winning clients' trust!
Dialogues/2061: System shows you've got a new mail.
Dialogues/2062: Looks like the townspeople already know you're here!
Dialogues/2063: Check it out to see what kind of task it is!
Dialogues/2064: After accepting a task, press [M] / to view the location on the map.
Dialogues/2071: Your name's probably spreading through the town now.
Dialogues/2072: You'll get more commissions like this in the future!
Dialogues/2073: You can access your Work Log by pressing [ESC].
Dialogues/2074: It records all your work progress.
Dialogues/2075: Besides assigned tasks, sometimes new tasks will show up on the bulletin board.
Dialogues/2076: Make sure to check it from time to time!
Dialogues/2081: It's getting late. New tasks won't come in until 6 AM.
Dialogues/2082: That's also when the town starts to wake up.
Dialogues/2083: Each day, townsfolk throw away some kinds of things—it's a pity to let it all go to waste.
Dialogues/2084: At night, everyone goes to rest. Take the chance to wander around the town...
Dialogues/2085: Or head home and get some sleep in your bed. Beep—
Dialogues/2090: The box I'm holding is linked to the company warehouse—no matter how big the item is, I can pull it out!
Dialogues/2091: You can also return things back into my box. (Left Click)
Dialogues/2100: Right click on the item held to change its direction.
Dialogues/2101: Scroll Wheel zooms the view, and hold Scroll Wheel to pan the view.
Dialogues/2110: Beep-boop. This room is all tidied up.
Dialogues/2111: Go check out other rooms. Hold Right Click at the door to enter.
Dialogues/2112: If you're holding anything, you can bring it along with you.
Dialogues/2120: Looks like you can right click to interact with this object.
Dialogues/2121: If you see an object with a yellow outline, interact with it and see what happens!
Dialogues/2131: Locations in the town are shown on this map. It is really convenient when you need to know where you are.
Dialogues/2132: I'll keep a scanned copy in my system so you can check it any time.
Dialogues/2134: Press [M]// whenever you're traveling through town to open the Map.
Dialogues/2140: Beep-boop. You have finished decorating your home.
Dialogues/2141: Feeling tired? You can sleep to pass the night.
Dialogues/2142: If you want to go out, you can click Complete Assignment or right-click and hold on the door.
Dialogues/2150: After hearing what happened to the landlord, my bestie moved in with me. I'm so happy!
Dialogues/2151: She insisted we install a phone—just in case some emergency happens.
Dialogues/2160: This record is from her. She's super into rock music.
Dialogues/2161: Sometimes she plays on the streets at night—you might run into her on a stroll.
Dialogues/2210: Beep—You can sleep in bed to end the day!
Dialogues/2221: Great, you've got a new house!
Dialogues/2250: Ah! That's the letter I wrote to Ian! (blushes)
Dialogues/2252: Eh… Why hasn't it been opened?
Dialogues/2258: (takes out a wooden box) Could you... help me put them all into this box? Thank you...
Dialogues/2300: Don't ask me, I don't remember what happened that night!
Dialogues/2302: You actually managed to find it...
Dialogues/2303: Darn—no, I mean, wow, you're amazing.

Dialogues/2309: Looks like we'll find the thief soon!

Dialogues/2316: Now the jewelry shop can reopen again.

Dialogues/2322: I've got a show soon, so I'm outta here!
Dialogues/2451: Everything's spilled all over the place.
Dialogues/2453: Guess I'll have to move things myself… huh!
Dialogues/2455: A guest is here. You are...?
Dialogues/2456: Ah—so Einsmart sent you to help out?
Dialogues/2457: That's fantastic! If I had to carry all this stuff by myself, it would've taken half my old life with it!
Dialogues/2458: I need to take a break. I'm exhausted after putting up just a few of these.
Dialogues/2460: Hey hey! Young one, wanna check out the scrapbooks I made?
Dialogues/2470: I made this toy with EinSmart, ho ho!
Dialogues/2471: This is the time capsule we buried together in our hometown a long time ago. He wrote his wishes in there.
Dialogues/2472: Good thing I remembered it, so I dug it out and brought it this time.
Dialogues/2473: He probably wrote that he wanted to become a scientist.
Dialogues/2474: What? It says he wants to always be with me?

Dialogues/2481: He used to love spacing out while staring at the sand.
Dialogues/2490: I bought some snacks for EinSmart.
Dialogues/2491: Don't wanna starve my dear grandson.
Dialogues/2500: This is his favorite movie when he was a child.
Dialogues/2501: I went all the way into town to get him this toy car.
Dialogues/2502: How time flies without you even noticing it!
Dialogues/2511: I also brought a tree seedling. I'm going to plant it in the backyard.
Dialogues/2512: There was one like it back at the old house—Einsmart used to hang all his wishes on it, hehe.
Dialogues/2513: But… my back's not what it used to be. Looks like I can't do the planting anymore.
Dialogues/2514: Can I ask you for this favor?
Dialogues/2520: This photo's gotten a bit old… I should take a new one with Einsmart someday.
Dialogues/2602: Twisted! Tangled! A spiral of time… oh, how beautiful she is...
Dialogues/2609: With it, we can change the past!
Dialogues/2610: Speaking of which, this door is now linked to the house I bought for Grandpa. It's just upstairs.
Dialogues/2611: Before Grandpa passed away, we spent a warm and lovely time there.
Dialogues/2612: Unfortunately, right when he moved in, I was caught up in a major project…
Dialogues/2613: He had to move everything by himself, and overworked himself into a serious illness.
Dialogues/2614: I always felt like his later health issues started with that one illness…
Dialogues/2615: And now, maybe I can finally make up for that regret.
Dialogues/2616: But I need to operate the portal, so I'm leaving the rest to you!
Dialogues/2617: Oh right, here's this <sprite="按键Icon" index=49>button for you.

Dialogues/2619: Alright, go interact with that door and check it out!
Dialogues/2701: You're such a good kid, too!
Dialogues/2710: Oh wow, you moved everything already? Thank you so much!
Dialogues/2711: Thank you on behalf of my grandson!
Dialogues/2720: I thought you dealt with the trash earlier...
Dialogues/2721: Oh wait, you're not the junk collector! You just look a little familiar...
Dialogues/2722: Einsmart is sleeping right now. He's very tired, so please be quiet when you leave, hoho!
Dialogues/2731: I miss you so much… Grandpa…
Dialogues/2741: Please wait patiently for Mr. Einsmart to upgrade my system.
Dialogues/2750: Hey! Still the same old me! (ノ•̀ω•́)ノ
Dialogues/2751: Since that whole incident, I've had to be more cautious with things.

Dialogues/2962: You look a lot like the person in my dream, ho!

Dialogues/3111: It feels like something is pulling at it.

Dialogues/3131: What are you snooping around my room for?

Dialogues/3170: You seem to always be around here.
Dialogues/3171: Do you like watching the sea too?

Dialogues/3190: There used to be a homeless guy on the street. Haven't seen him lately… did he find a home?

Dialogues/3210: So what if I accidentally fished out all the park's fish! Now they put up signs just to stop me! Rude!
Dialogues/3211: Guess I'll sneak in at midnight from now on~

Dialogues/3230: Do you know the robot Mark? He was invented by the town's scientist.

Dialogues/3251: Perhaps we were never meant to sail too far or explore too deep.
Dialogues/3260: The beach always washes up something.
Dialogues/3261: If only I had a little shovel, I might dig up something interesting.
Dialogues/3270: You have to keep your imagination in check.
Dialogues/3280: Through the messy graffiti, you can vaguely make out the words—"Waiting to Dream."
Dialogues/3312: But it's actually a good omen.

Dialogues/3331: Please don't interrupt us on duty!
Dialogues/3341: Did that statue's eye just move?
Dialogues/3362: <shock a=0.5>Never gonna run around and desert you~

Dialogues/3381: (Muttering to oneself, or maybe talking to someone)
Dialogues/3391: Why did I end up in the cemetery again?
Dialogues/3392: Feels like… it's a habit of mine?
Dialogues/3403: I hope one day I can write something that resonates like that too.

Dialogues/3452: Just walking past her shop, the smell draws your nose in!

Dialogues/3472: Looks like it's out of power!
Dialogues/3481: There I met an old lady who was kind of scary.

Dialogues/3491: Because you have a heart that shines like a gem.

Dialogues/3502: The slippers at home sometimes go missing.

Dialogues/3513: It didn't rain today, but I brought an umbrella. I win.

Dialogues/3515: Didn't I just walk past here?
Dialogues/3516: Someone's playing guitar in the hallway again.
Dialogues/3517: Just missed the bus. Well, guess it's fate.

Dialogues/3520: Just a secret—those glowing eyes in the bushes aren't always a cat!

Dialogues/3522: I feel like someone's watching me.
Dialogues/3523: They say a falling star means someone has left this world.
Dialogues/3524: I have some very close friends—we eat together, sleep together, dress up, and go out together, almost as if we were one person.
Dialogues/3525: Whenever you appear, I feel like someone else is watching too.
Dialogues/3526: Tentacles peeking from the manhole? Must've been my imagination.

Dialogues/3530: If I learn alchemy, will I have infinite gold?

Dialogues/3602: This is going to be a big project…

Dialogues/5341: (The cat stuck out its tongue at you and ran away.)
Dialogues/5381: (The cat stuck out its tongue at you and ran away.)

Dialogues/5417: …I think I'm going insane. Can ghosts even go crazy?

Dialogues/5598: As if we had just played together yesterday!

Dialogues/5710: You the new caregiver? Close the window for me, will you?
Dialogues/5711: Those "thieves" have been getting bold lately, backed by someone big!
Dialogues/5712: See? My medicine bottle's uncovered again!
Dialogues/5713: That guy climbed in through the window and stole it!
Dialogues/5722: These things... suddenly reminded me of her...
Dialogues/5732: These things... suddenly reminded me of her...
Dialogues/5736: You probably don't realize how important the item you brought back is.
Dialogues/5737: I found the right person after all—thank you!
Dialogues/5741: Amazing, <PlayerName>! Only trusted employees are assigned to decorate model rooms!
Dialogues/5742: To meet the needs of target clients, we usually need to place specific items in the model rooms.
Dialogues/5743: This time's specific item is already in my pocket—go ahead and pull it out!
Dialogues/5747: Got it! The target client might be a streamer!
Dialogues/5748: But just these things aren't enough—beep!
Dialogues/5749: Try opening the 【Storage】 and 【Decorate】 buttons on the lower left.
Dialogues/5750: In the Storage, you'll find test items provided by the company—any item you've unlocked can be used unlimitedly!
Dialogues/5751: After clicking the Decorate button (highlighted), move the cursor over an object to change its color—give it a try, beep!
Dialogues/5805: We kept waiting, but he never came back.
Dialogues/5816: When I was little, I used to be here...
Dialogues/5818: It feels like I lost something very precious.
Dialogues/5819: There must be something that can help me recall those childhood memories.
Dialogues/5832: You must still have many questions in your heart.
Dialogues/5833: Now, I will tell you the truth.
Dialogues/5834: This place… was originally a project I abandoned during development.
Dialogues/5840: Perhaps you've already noticed, the inscriptions in the stone hall do not match the current state of the town.
Dialogues/5841: As the manager of this project, I didn't want to admit failure…
Dialogues/5842: But the town was a project on the verge of termination.
Dialogues/5843: Yet, even after losing outside support, the town did not stop as expected. Instead, it began to develop on its own.
Dialogues/5844: The townsfolk gradually strayed from the paths written in the inscriptions, spawning countless unforeseen branches.
Dialogues/5845: New shoots tangled with predetermined fate, bringing about innumerable anomalies.
Dialogues/5846: I tried desperately to mend them, but I could never catch up with the speed at which the program crumbled.
Dialogues/5847: Bit by bit, I lost control over the town… until—
Dialogues/5849: At first, I thought you were just another error that needed fixing.
Dialogues/5850: I even tested you, to learn your origins and intentions.
Dialogues/5851: But soon I realized—you could resolve anomalies I could never touch.
Dialogues/5852: You could stabilize branches that were about to collapse.
Dialogues/5853: You helped the townsfolk put their lives back in order. I believe you also have the power to make the town even better.
Dialogues/5854: Perhaps…it's time for me to let go.
Dialogues/5855: I will entrust you with the remaining authority—
Dialogues/5856: the power to control weather and time.
Dialogues/5857: The town is now in your hands. Take good care of it.
Dialogues/5858: To me, this is also a happy ending.
Dialogues/5859: Maybe I will come back here again.
Dialogues/5860: Just woke up… ah! You're already here!
Dialogues/5861: Sorry… I drank too much again last night, and the bar is a mess.
Dialogues/5862: Jane already told me you'd be coming, so I'll leave the cleanup to you.
Dialogues/5863: Next time, I'll make you my signature cocktail! I've got to run.
Dialogues/5902: The nurse said they're going to assign me a roommate.
Dialogues/5911: I don't know why, but I kind of miss her…
Dialogues/5920: Every individual is a lonely island...
Dialogues/5921: Huh? How did you get here?
Dialogues/5922: I thought I was about to start a castaway's life...
Dialogues/5923: Since we've met, you can take this money.
Dialogues/5924: See you again if fate allows.
Quest/任务面板下小提示_完成基础任务后敲章即可离开: Stamp to exit once tasks are completed.
Quest/古德豪斯镇长的信内容:
Hello! I'm Goodman Goodhouse, the mayor of Whisper Town. Welcome to our beautiful town! 

Whisper Town is a warm and peaceful place, and I'm sure you'll soon fall in love with it. Your new neighbors will likely come to you for help before long, and I hope your arrival will bring a fresh touch to our community. 

Once again, welcome to the big family of our town!
Quest/Promise: I acknowledge and confirm that our company is fully committed to meeting every client's needs!
Quest/房间已经收拾完成 去看看别的房间吧: This room is all tidied up. Let's go check out the other rooms.
Quest/接下来把车子里的东西都拿出来吧: Let's take everything out of the car.
Quest/SuitTitle_0: Want a studio big enough to hold all my specimens
Quest/SuitDescription_0: Even before moving to this town, I'd already decided—this time, I'm going to make a space where I can properly store all my collections. Dried flowers, feathers, insect bones, and old books I've read over and over again... The room should have a sense of age, but not feel gloomy— Ideally, the moment you walk in, you'd smell old paper and the dry scent of wild grass.
Quest/SuitTitle_1: Help me find a comfy spot for my instruments!
Quest/SuitDescription_1: I brought my piano, guitar, and speakers, but this place is a mess. Could you help me tidy it up? Nothing too flashy—just something cozy and pleasant. It'd be great if we could split it into a practice area and a recording zone.
Quest/SuitTitle_2: I want to decorate a little fisherman's hut—hehe.
Quest/SuitDescription_2: I go fishing whenever I have time, and when I don't, I like sorting my gear indoors. This time, I picked out a room just to show off my rods and specimens, and I've got some proud fishing photos I'd love to hang on the wall! Hope this space brings me good luck from the sea—hehe...
Quest/SuitTitle_3: Can you help me organize my cobbler's shop?
Quest/SuitDescription_3: Lately I've been so stressed out... Nails, brushes, leather, thread—they're all piled together, and I can't find anything. Someone please help me get this space back in order so I can fix shoes in peace. I'll pay well—thank you so much!
Quest/SuitDescription_4: I haven't had anywhere to work out since moving, and my fitness soul is crying! I'm urgently looking for someone to help me set up a home gym. I want to lay out all my equipment. I can't wait any longer—I need to move my body now!
Quest/SuitTitle_5: Beep—help me build my gaming zone!
Quest/SuitDescription_5: My dream is to have a cozy room where I can game all day at home. My console, cartridges, controllers—they're all here. Can someone help me design the perfect setup?
Quest/SuitTitle_6: Wouldn't it be lovely to have a little flower shop?
Quest/SuitDescription_6: Every morning, I go to the flower market to pick fresh-cut blooms and fill my home with fragrance. Now, I'm thinking of opening a breezy, sunny flower shop. I need a space to wrap bouquets and a cozy corner for guests to rest. I don't know much about interior design—can you help me?
Quest/SuitTitle_7: I wish I could return to childhood just once.
Quest/SuitDescription_7: I spent all my summer holidays at my grandma's country house, but now the old house and the well in the yard are gone. Time won't turn back, but I saved a few old things when I moved: grapevine, ceiling fan, wicker chairs... Could you help me recreate the room from my memories?
Quest/SuitTitle_8: I want a little forest cabin of my own.
Quest/SuitDescription_8: I always watch those treehouse-building videos—seeing people chop wood and light fires in nature—it's so dreamy! Now I finally have the chance, and I'd love your help to turn this room into a quiet, nature-filled place where I can daydream and do nothing.
Quest/SuitTitle_9: I want to live in a room with a Chinese aesthetic.
Quest/SuitDescription_9: I admire the stillness, balance, and blank space in Chinese ink paintings. My room doesn't have to be complex—as long as it has elegance and a bit of Zen, I'll be happy. If we could use fine wood, even better. Once it's done, I'll invite you over for tea and art appreciation.
Quest/SuitDescription_10: The true lady of this room is actually my cat—I'm just the two-legged servant in charge of her litter box. There are toys and snacks all over the floor; I can barely walk through it. Please help me turn it into a room where I can lie down and cuddle my cat!
Quest/SuitDescription_11: I have too many dolls, and there's no room left. I'd like you to design a room that can display all of them. Being surrounded by my plushie would be the best. I'm definitely taking lots of cute photos when it's done!
Quest/SuitTitle_12: Please build me a magical fortune-telling room
Quest/SuitDescription_12: I've actually been studying star charts, crystal balls, candles, and stuff… Now I want a space where I can meditate, divine, and read tarot cards. It has to keep a sense of mystery—that's how I channel my magical energy.
Quest/SuitTitle_13: I need a quiet detective office
Quest/SuitDescription_13: Can't afford office rent… so I plan to turn this old house into a "detective agency." A real detective needs a clue wall and some file boxes. Oh, and I need a coffee machine. No caffeine, no work!
Quest/SuitTitle_14: Help me set up a proper streaming room
Quest/SuitDescription_14: As a gaming streamer, I sometimes get a little too loud when I'm hyped. I really hope this room can be soundproofed—I don't want to get any more noise complaints!
Quest/SuitTitle_15: Don't ask what this room is for
Quest/SuitDescription_15: I've been getting into antiques lately, and once I start picking them out, I just can't stop. Ceramic, bronze, paper, wood… I'm worried people will think I've lost it if they see, so don't make it look too flashy. And no, I never said it's a collection room!
Quest/SuitTitle_16: I want to hide here on weekends and do some woodworking
Quest/SuitDescription_16: There's something about touching sawdust and working with wood that makes me feel at peace. I'm looking for someone to design a workshop where I can enjoy my favorite hobby when I have time.
Quest/SuitDescription_17: For me, bathing is the soul of a home. Everything else is secondary. It's not just about washing—it's about restoring peace inside. Ending the day right is important to me, so I want someone who understands to take this on.
Quest/SuitTitle_18: I want to live in an animated summer
Quest/SuitDescription_18: Like the ones from childhood cartoons—fizzy soda bubbling, electric fan humming, wind chimes swaying by the window, cicadas buzzing outside. If you can make that happen, contact me now!
Quest/SuitDescription_19: I only meant to help Cheryl out at first, but I ended up falling in love with baking. Now my home is filled with the scent of butter. Can you help me organize the kitchen? I want to work on a new vanilla cake recipe.
Quest/SuitTitle_20: Looking for a cozy cocktail bar design
Quest/SuitDescription_20: I want a bar counter with great vibes where I can mix all kinds of beautiful cocktails. Just a few high stools, some favorite records, and it'll be perfect for chatting with friends until sunrise—or sitting alone, enjoying the breeze.
Quest/SuitTitle_21: Help me set up a plant lab
Quest/SuitDescription_21: As a plant researcher, I've chosen this room as a light experiment lab. Every plant needs the right temperature and lighting. Now the house is full of soil, pots, and equipment! If anyone can take on this interior job, I'll gift them a rare herb I grew myself.
Quest/SuitDescription_22: Our town's film club screens a movie every Thursday, but we've never had a dedicated space. I want to build a cozy, spacious home theater where we can project onto the whole wall and really get immersed in the movie magic.
Quest/SuitDescription_23: A few of us started a garage band, but we've never had a proper place to rehearse. Now that we found this room, could you help us turn it into a rebellious rock space? Just enough room for a drum set, speakers, and a few electric guitars would be great.
Quest/SuitDescription_24: I love books so much, they've started to pile up everywhere—even under my bed. So I dreamed of turning this room into my own home library. I want a full wall of books and a peaceful reading spot to spend every afternoon lost in pages. If you can help me make this happen, I'll be so grateful!
Quest/SuitDescription_25: I love camping and hiking—wandering, resting, watching the mountains and rivers. This room is one of my rare resting spots, and it's important to me. It's piled with maps, binoculars, tents, water bottles… all my travel buddies. I need someone reliable to help me organize it all.
Quest/SuitDescription_26: The anime club is just a bunch of like-minded folks who get together to talk about anime and re-create iconic scenes. This meeting space means a lot to us, but it's become a mess—wigs, props, and costumes are all over. We'd love your help to tidy it up while keeping the cosplay vibes alive!
Quest/SuitDescription_27: I'm a coffee lover who's collected lots of imported beans. So I figured, why not open a cozy little coffee stand? One with a relaxed vibe where townsfolk can sit, chat, and enjoy the aroma of freshly brewed beans.
Quest/SuitDescription_28: I've been writing web novels for years—never had many readers, but this time inspiration really hit me! So I need a calm writing space: soft chair, thick blackout curtains, and no interruptions. Don't break my focus! If this novel becomes a hit, you'll get all the credit!
Quest/SuitDescription_29: The sun sets behind the city skyline, I raise my wine glass and watch TV—this is the life I want.
Quest/SuitDescription_30: I'm a business woman. Coming back to hometown this time, I just want to create more jobs and bring prosperity to the town, hehe.
Quest/SuitTitle_31: Time to start a healthy life
Quest/SuitDescription_31: A healthy life needs a strong body. You! Right now! One-two-three-four—breathe, stretch! Arms wide open, full of energy!

Mail/153: —— Good News from GD Real Estate ——
Mail/42:
"After serving the town for many years, the Brilliant Stars Jewelry Store was forced to close for over a month following a burglary. Thanks to the help of kind-hearted locals, the stolen gems were eventually recovered, and the store's security system was fully upgraded, making its reopening possible.

At the reopening ceremony, shop owner Sparky gave special thanks to housekeeper <PlayerName>: 'Without <PlayerName>, we might not have made it through.'"
Mail/46:
"After the previous break-in, Brilliant Stars Jewelry Store had planned to cease operations. However, thanks to the help of supportive townsfolk, it reopened with hope for a fresh start. But the good times didn't last—once again, the store became the target of thieves. It is reported that all four 'Love of the Rainbow' gems were stolen in the latest heist.

With a bitter smile, Sparky remarked, 'The Love of the Rainbow isn't just a set of gems—it's the very heart of Brilliant Stars and of me. Without them, there's no reason for this store to keep going.'"
Mail/44:
"Although the Brilliant Stars Jewelry Store reopened after the previous burglary, many longtime customers were surprised to find that the store's four treasured gems, known as the 'Love of the Rainbow,' were no longer complete.

With foot traffic steadily declining, the store may soon be forced to shut its doors once again.

Fighting back tears, Sparky said, 'Without the Love of the Rainbow, it feels like the soul of the store is gone. Maybe… this really is the end.'"

Mail/94: A wandering sculptor once stayed briefly in the town and left behind several white, lifelike statues that stood quietly in various corners. After the sculptor left, the mayor decided to gather the statues and display them in the park. Since then, whenever night falls and silence descends, the sculptures begin to awaken—moving slowly and making soft, rustling noises. Occasionally, a faint glow emerges from the arch ruins in the middle of the lake, and strange creatures of unusual shapes walk out from within.
Mail/152:
A wellness tea brewing class is held every weekend.
Spend more time playing mahjong with the elders.
Mail/1:
According to ancient texts, this is an exceptionally old magic circle, said to be capable of sending not only objects but even living beings to a dimension beyond the reality of this world.

During the casting, the target must stand at the exact center of the circle for the sigils to activate. At the moment of initiation, the lines emit a distinct surge of energy—an omen that the circle has begun to operate.

Countless scholars have tried to record its workings, yet none have been able to uncover its destination. On rare occasions, some have succeeded in casting it, and witnesses claim to have seen them vanish into the glow of the sigils—where they went is unknown, and they never returned.
Mail/66: As a token of appreciation for the players' support, we have directly credited 3000 special coupons to your account, please check it in-game!
Mail/119: Cheryl manages the daily affairs of the alchemy lab and is also the personal assistant to alchemist Ian. Though she doesn't have the qualifications to conduct experiments, Ian trusts her more than anyone else. Cooking is her passion, and even when Ian is so absorbed in his work that he forgets to eat or sleep, she still prepares a hot meal for them both. Over the years, she has quietly protected this place filled with magic.
Mail/150: Congratulations on renovating your property. From now on, you can freely change the layout inside your room.
Mail/82: Congratulations! You've been officially promoted to [新职位/等级名称]! Your hard work and performance have been highly recognized by the company—we're proud of you. To celebrate, we're awarding you [金额] in voucher. Keep up the great work—more challenges await you ahead!
Mail/58:
Cute cat, huh?
You're pretty reliable. The key to that empty house in town is your tip.
Be good to my cat!
Mail/114: Dr. Einsmart has never forgiven himself for burying himself in work during his grandfather's final days, failing to spend time with or care for him properly. That powerless regret gradually turned into uncontrollable rage—he became obsessed with making homemade bombs, always wearing a white lab coat stained with gunpowder and muttering to himself, as if ready to take the whole town down with him. According to the Whisper Daily, this "Demolition Maniac" scientist has already blown his own roof off three times.
Mail/83: Exciting News from the HR Department
Mail/70: Exclusive Interview with the Legendary Alchemist! Is the Higher-Dimensional World Real??
Mail/137: Finlord is a restless troublemaker. Under the secret instigation of Eltz, he committed all kinds of misdeeds around town. Disguising his actions as "installing surveillance," he secretly placed energy collectors in every room of residents' homes. He also covertly tampered with the alchemy workshop's magic circle, trapping Ian inside. At the hospital, in an attempt to steal Lady Bai's belongings, he even chased her cat all the way to the garden, throwing the entire town into chaos.

Mail/92: Ian was a sorcerer obsessed with alchemy, devoting his life to the pursuit of transcendence beyond human limits. Driven by obsession, he secretly sacrificed his closest assistant, Cheryl, attempting to step into a higher dimension through a forbidden ritual. The ritual ultimately failed. Ian's body collapsed into a two-dimensional form and turned to stone, his consciousness forever trapped within the altar he had drawn by his own hand—a curse that can never be erased.

Mail/55:
In recent weeks, I've felt an indescribable pulling sensation whenever I pass certain spots in town or at home—as if a faint, stubborn magnetic field is trying to steer my thoughts.

This morning, as I passed the museum again, I noticed the number above it had silently changed. I can't remember when it happened, only that the pull has gotten stronger.

I'm beginning to suspect that the town's memories, people's behaviors, even my dreams, are being subtly "rewritten" by these fields.

Mail/17:
On August 23, the resident of an apartment at 7 Whisper Street reported unusual activity. Police found a hidden room beside the bedroom and a homeless man inside, later sending him to a shelter.

He had been living in an abandoned room next to the owner's, entering at night through the bathroom. Police found makeshift supplies and a work ID, but neither the man nor the company existed in town.
Mail/19: On August 23rd, the resident of a rental house on Whisper Street No. 7 reported unusual activity inside their home. Upon arrival, police discovered a hidden room adjacent to the bedroom, where they found a homeless man hiding. He was removed from the premises immediately. According to reports, this man had been secretly entering the abandoned space at night through a hidden passage in the bathroom. When found, he was dressed in tattered clothes and appeared unkempt. The room was filled with makeshift living supplies, suggesting long-term occupancy. The man has since been taken to a shelter for assistance.
Mail/98: One day, a fish-headed creature brought him seven emotion energy orbs. With those, he repaired his spaceship and left. Since then, the museum has become a tourist hotspot, and some residents even miss the emotionless days of the past.
Mail/122: Original Design Notes: During her hospitalization, the stray cat that spent every day with Lady Bai was actually an alien lifeform. After Lady Bai passed away, the alien cat used its powers to preserve her soul, transforming it into a litter of newborn kittens.

Mail/53:
Recently, shop owner Miki and his furniture store "IKA Home" have returned to the spotlight. After several months, Miki has finally released a new set of woodcarving works. While these pieces may not match the flawless perfection of his award-winning era, they carry a special charm.

We spoke with many customers, and they all expressed that they could once again feel the unique spirit in Miki's carvings.

Some carving enthusiasts asked Miki for his secret and requested recommendations for carving tools. He simply smiled and held up a modest carving knife, saying, "Tools and technique don't matter. What truly matters is the emotion you put in and your desire to create."  
It is said that this carving knife was the first one gifted to him by his master when he began learning the craft.
Mail/48:
Recently, townsfolk noticed a glowing number "42" above the museum.

It attracted a large crowd of people, sparking widespread speculation and chaos, until the police had to intervene.

After everyone left, the number was still there...

Mail/129:
Sage is a thrill-seeking journalist with a strong sense of justice—brave, upright, and always the first to rush into danger. He's never afraid of conspiracy or chaos.
He's skilled at exposing the truth through his camera and has a knack for easing tension with humor.
Though his actions can be a bit clumsy and occasionally lead to awkward moments, his intentions remain pure, and he's always on the front line of the story.
Mail/8: Seeking several movie attendants to provide excellent service and ensure a pleasant, smooth experience for theatergoers. Please inquire at the front desk for more information—we'd love to have you join us!
Mail/12:
Several months ago, Mr. Elts made a remarkable discovery—an ancient ruin in the southwest of town. Since then, ongoing archaeological excavations have unearthed a wealth of treasures, including antiques, artworks, and even fossils! 

Our devoted cultural preservation team is now working tirelessly to clean and catalog these precious finds. In just one week, as the museum unveils its refreshed new look, these beautiful relics of the past will finally be on display. We warmly invite you to experience the wonder of history woven into every artifact!

Mail/69: The familiar scent of flowers returns to the corners of Whisper Town—Anna's Flower Shop has officially reopened today after a period of renovation and upgrades! The newly revamped shop has a fresh look. In addition to offering a variety of elegant potted plants, it now features a dedicated section for seeds and gardening tools, perfect for garden lovers who dream from "planting" to "blossoming." Some mysterious seeds have also appeared on the shelves. They may look ordinary, but they hold hidden surprises. Perhaps rare species, perhaps never-before-seen fantastical plants. The answer awaits your planting.

Mail/96: The museum curator is not human. His spaceship once crash-landed here due to a malfunction, and he stayed in the town temporarily.
Mail/61:
The museum's recent new exhibits have received widespread praise. However, some have raised questions: with such a large building, is it not wasteful to restrict access to just the first floor while sealing off the second?

In response, Museum Director Elts stated, "The second floor of the museum is my personal office and is currently not open to the public. I apologize for the inconvenience."

When asked why such a large office space was necessary, the director quickly excused himself and retreated into the password-protected room on the second floor.
Mail/54: The Mystery of the Museum Numbers

"Without my grandfather, I might not have persisted on the path of science so long. The portal can be connected to more places, haha, if you're dissatisfied with the past, come use my portal to change it!"

Mail/71:
The town's alchemist who mysteriously disappeared years ago has recently made a miraculous return. According to his girlfriend, he vanished after enthusiastically activating some mysterious magic circle in his room.

The alchemist revealed that he had accidentally entered a truly existing higher-dimensional world. In that world, he could clearly see everyday scenes from the town—including his beloved girlfriend crying for him day and night.

He searched desperately for a way back in vain, but fortunately, with the help of some mysterious force, he finally returned to the town safely.

Mail/103:
This is a marvelous utopia. To the south lies a soft, sandy beach and an endless blue ocean. The northern forest constantly provides timber and adventure. In the northwest, golden wheat fields and peaceful pastures supply the town with abundant organic food.
There are no office buildings here—because no one needs to work. Every resident lives in an orderly rhythm, guided by their own pace and rules.
In this town, being a police officer is considered a retirement job. The most "serious" cases might be a dog pooping at a neighbor's doorstep or a creeping vine blocking someone's sunlight.
This is a place where everyone can find their ideal way of life—a truly perfect town.
Mail/97: This species gains energy by feeding on the emotions of the townspeople.

Mail/37:
Yesterday afternoon, one of the most coveted assets of the famous "Sunglasses Gang" — a luxurious property in the town center — was unexpectedly inherited by a housekeeper. 

The estate, long desired by real estate agencies and high society alike, has now stirred public uproar and disbelief. Kuroi, a core figure of the gang, declined to answer any questions from reporters. According to hospital nurses, he has been seen frequently sitting on a bench in the hospital garden recently.
Mail/35:
Yesterday morning, the leader of the well-known gang "Dessert Gang," Kuroi, led more than 30 members in a raid on St. Vladimir Hospital.

According to on-site interviews, Kuroi claimed that his mother mysteriously disappeared during her hospitalization and suspected that the hospital was deliberately hiding her for the purpose of human experimentation.

After repeated demands for an explanation were ignored, Kuroi and his gang forcibly entered the hospital to search the premises, resulting in a clash with medical staff and security. Seven people were injured in the incident.
Mail/134:
You're brave—you weren't scared off by those things and climbed all the way up here.

You're safe now. Take a moment to enjoy the fruits of your effort.

But… unfortunately, what you were hoping for isn't here.

No treasure, no surprise—nothing at all.
Mail/140:
You've helped us a lot. 
Since I'm living with Luna now, I'll just let you rent my old place for free. As for the lease… 10,000 years ( ^_- )☆ 
No need to thank me too much!

Image/鱼人任务档案4: Energy Research Report Once energy collection is complete, the spaceship can launch—but at the cost of destroying the town.
Image/鱼人任务: Mission Codename: "Art Is Dead" Target: The artist Miki Objective: Eliminate Miki's creative drive Details: Fishbrain, your mission is to secretly replace his sculpting knife with our specially made Dependency Blade when he's not looking. Observe if he develops an addiction and report back to me immediately.
Image/鱼人任务档案2: Mission Codename: "Operation Gemstone" Target: Gem shop owner Sparky Details: Install the energy transfer device inside the gem shop. Security is tight, so remember to use some disguise! Outcome: Failed You fish-brain! Couldn't you at least pretend to be from the power company and walk in like you belong there?! And you even got into a fight with the staff—are you trying to give me a heart attack? Second Attempt Outcome: Success
Image/鱼人任务档案3: Mission Codename: "Switch and Replace" Targets: Luna, Alchemist Ian, Furniture Shop Owner Miki, and Old Lady Bai from the hospital Details: Learn from the failure at the gem shop—no more mistakes this time. We can't afford to stir up too much attention in the town! Outcome: Success
Image/鱼人任务档案1: Mission Codename: "Underground" Target: Scientist EinSmart Details: Sneak into EinSmart's basement and steal the energy transfer device. Make sure to act while he's sleeping—I've learned that his schedule is completely flipped, so he's an easy target! Outcome: Success
Image/鱼人过场2: A friend lives beneath the horizon
Image/科学家设计稿1: Achieve this dazzling miracle in human history! Go to a place humanity has never been——the past!
Image/鱼人日记珠宝店: After several days of relentless observation, I finally discovered a security flaw in this shop. Bop bop bop.
Image/鱼人过场4: And I melt myself into blue imagination
Image/奶奶日记结尾10.16:
August 13, Rainy

Kuroi seems to be waiting for my orders, but I no longer have the strength to give any. 

The cat climbed up the tree to see me, stood at the window for a while, but didn't come in. 

It's time to pass on my belongings to someone worthy.
Image/种子阶段滴下对应的营养液 等待一段时间 就能看到培育出的品种啦: During the seed stage, drop the corresponding nutrient solution and wait for a while—you'll soon see the variety you've cultivated~
Image/栽培指南2: During the seed stage, drop the corresponding nutrient solution. After a while, you'll see what kind of plant you've grown.
Image/鱼人过场3: Each dusk, the sea breeze taps the window frame
Image/若是植物不小心枯死 或是种出了不想要的作物 可以用铲子把他铲掉哦: If a plant accidentally dies or produces a crop you don't want, you can use a shovel to remove it.
Image/栽培指南4: If a plant withers by accident, or you grow something you don't want, use a shovel to remove it.
Image/星球游戏:
In the lofty light above the scholar's podium  
Beneath the jaws of the wall-dwelling creature  
Within the embrace of ancient greenery  
In the crevice between star-lit stone giants
Image/科学家设计稿3-1: Install all three Bloch Spheres at the same time to unlock the chest.
Image/奶奶日记结尾10.15:
July 22, Rainy

Couldn't get up from the bed. 

The cat food just arrived—too slow! 

The soup Kuroi made was so bland. I'll cook it myself next time.
Image/科学家设计稿2-2:
Major flaw: The lever is prone to damage and loss.
Now the phase ball inside can't be taken out!
Image/奶奶日记结尾10.11:
March 5, Sunny

Coughing. 

Today marks the tenth anniversary of the organization. Kuroi asked me to take a photo at the old base. 

The cat didn't come back to its bed tonight—probably went to the rooftop again.
Image/奶奶日记结尾10.12:
May 22, Cloudy

Feeling a bit heavy. 

The kitten refuses to drink goat milk, and I'm not ready to take the meds either. 

Bought new cat food today.
Image/奶奶日记结尾10.14:
May 30, Overcast

It's cold. The wind is loud. 

Kuroi came to see me. Asking him where the cat went is pointless—he never finds the cat anyway.
Image/馆长给鱼人的任务:
New Message: Elts
Finlord, a few days ago I gave the office on the second floor of the museum a thorough cleaning. When you have time, please help me put everything back in place—just make sure not to go during the day.
Image/请等待他长大吧: Please wait for it to grow!
Image/栽培指南3: Please wait for it to grow~
Image/奶奶日记2: Put enough cat food in the basket
Image/科学家设计稿2-3:
Simple mechanical structure!
The foundation of the world's operation!
Image/鱼人字条:
Thank you,  
stranger who helped me find the pill.  
I've now remembered everything about myself. But since you've uncovered my secret, I'm sorry—I'll have to find you and erase your memory.
Image/老太太的信:
The day I die will be the day the Sunglasses Gang disbands.
Those who owe me their lives—I'll no longer hold them accountable.
Kuroi, please bury my sunglasses by my left hand.
As for the cat, make sure it goes to someone smart and capable.
Image/鱼人字条炼金师: These books and materials can now help fulfill your wish.
Image/科学家设计稿2-1: Think positively, the contents are absolutely safe until the lever is found! New invention——EinSmart Safe!
Image/幼儿园寄信1:
To Mother Boss:
Today marks the tenth anniversary of the organization. Thank you for your strict guidance over the years. I've always regarded you as my real mother. No matter where you are in the future, I will wear my sunglasses with pride at all times. I won't let anyone tarnish the honor of the Sunglasses Gang.
Image/科学家设计稿3-2: Too bad a Bloch Sphere is missing. This chest will be forever locked...
Image/科学家设计稿3-3: Wait! It can never be opened! EinSmart Safe 2.0!
Image/白老太信:
The day I die will be the day the Sunglasses Gang disbands.
Those who owe me their lives—I'll no longer hold them accountable.
Kuroi, please bury my sunglasses by my left hand.
As for the cat, make sure it goes to someone smart.
Image/白老太墓碑: May the light never fade, and your kindness remain forever
Image/爷爷墓碑空: Looks like there's a missing photo here.
Image/爷爷墓碑: In memory of the best grandpa in the world
Image/员工墓碑: Don't stop me, I can still do this

QuestLetter/21:
Hi! I'm Luna. 

I just came to Whisper Town. It's been a little hard to sleep. Thankfully, my dog, Nyla, came with me. She spends the day sniffing around and curls up next to me at night. And my luggage finally arrived! Having familiar things around makes both of us feel so much better. 

Could you help me move in? I'd be super grateful, and Nyla will be right there cheering you on!

QuestLetter/31: Hello, this is Sparky, the store manager of 'Brilliant Star'. If you've been following the news lately, you probably already know why I'm contacting you, so I'll keep it brief. Due to a recent burglary at the store, I need someone to help tidy up inside, install a security system, and convert the basement storage into a workspace. Please come asap, I really need your help!

QuestLetter/41:
I'm tired. Maybe it's time to go back home.

There's a little cabin by the sea—the place I used to call home when I was a child.

Could you go and check on it for me?
QuestLetter/42:

QuestLetter/50:
This is Ian's workshop, he was an incredible alchemist. I still remember how he once stood at the door, eyes shining, telling me he was about to activate a grand magic circle. He never came out of the workshop after that day.

All these years, I've kept the place exactly as it was... always thinking he might return. But now, I think... it's time to let go.

I'm going to seal this place for good. Before I leave, would you help me clean it one last time? And after that, please take good care of this house for me. Thank you.

I wish he could've pulled himself together and kept it going.

QuestLetter/111:
Hi, you might not know me, but you've definitely read my articles. I'm Sage, the smartest reporter at Whisper Town Daily. 

I set up a chess room in my office, and the local seniors often come by to chat. (Don't ask why!) 

The next Mahjong King Tournament will be held there soon, but I've been busy tailing a suspicious figure and haven't had time to clean or decorate. I heard you're a home care expert—think you can handle it?

Thank you. The other day I returned to the cabin and saw the room all neat and tidy—it finally felt a little like it used to.

I took some time to repaint the walls, and this color brings a quiet calm to my heart… Lately, I've needed some time to slowly sort through my thoughts.

I brought quite a few things with me. Could you give me a hand moving them inside?

QuestLetter/221:
Hi there! My 24/7 convenience store is almost ready to open! I've stocked up on all kinds of goodies. 

I want this place to be a stop for everyone. But to be honest, it's been tough handling everything by myself. 

I heard you've helped a few folks around town, so I came to ask for your help too! Let's make the grand opening of "Convience Store No. 8" a lively one!

QuestLetter/161: The day has come! It has finally arrived! The truth of this universe! All the mysteries of time! The one and only Time Portal is finally complete! My dear friend, I couldn't have done all of this without you. Now, let's go back to the past! And Back! To! The! Future!

QuestLetter/251:
The laws of time are trembling in my hands. Returning to the past is no longer a fantasy, it's a miracle on the verge of becoming real. 

After countless experiments, I've made a significant breakthrough. Now, everything is ready. The first real application is about to begin. 

Head to the underground lab beneath my grandpa's room—I've set up a temporary workspace. There's something crucial I need your help with! I'll be waiting for you there.

QuestLetter/261:
Hi! It's Luna back again! 

Have you read the latest newspaper? You really saved me last time. Thank you so much! Oh, and that secret room has already gone—I turned it into a part of the bedroom, creating a larger space. 

My bestie has been talking about living together for years, and now it's finally possible! She's arriving any minute, but the house is still messy. I can't manage it by myself—especially with Nyla jumping on and off the boxes and messing up the blankets I just folded. So… I'm counting on you!

QuestLetter/270: Recently, a large amount of scattered furniture was found on the town's coast, suspected to be spilled cargo from a recent shipwreck. Residents found many pieces of furniture on the sandy shore, making for quite a peculiar sight. The shipping company that caused the accident has since gone bankrupt, and the drifting furniture is temporarily unclaimed, becoming a notable scene of the seaside.
QuestLetter/311:
Hello, I'm Anna. 

I've always felt that plants speak a romantic language. My wish is to fill Whisper Town's breeze with the scent of flowers. 

Now, my flower shop finally has a chance to be renovated. And you—our town's most trusted home organizer! I'd love to entrust this dream shop to you. Please help tidy it up, and let the fragrance flow from the door to the whole street. My flowers are blooming just for you!

QuestLetter/341:
Hey, what's up? 

I'm Jane, planning to open a punk rock-style instrument store right in the heart of town. On opening day, I'm throwing a blazing underground show to honor my idols—the Rolling Bones! 

But before that, I need to move in a piano, speakers, a drum set, and a whole bunch of guitars... Ugh, there's barely any time left to rehearse. I heard you're a moving pro—hit me up when you're free!

Hi there, I'm Cheryl.

I just rented that little shop by the street and plan to turn it into a cozy bakery, selling sweet treats to people passing by. It's not a big space, but I hope everyone who walks in can feel a bit of happiness in the air~ 

I'd love your help getting this little dream started and setting everything up.
QuestLetter/361:
Hi there, I'm a friend of Miki. 

Lately, Miki's been telling me that a bunch of kittens have been causing quite a commotion in the supermarket. They're getting into everything and seriously affecting business. We're also worried they might get hurt while chasing each other around the furniture. 

Could you help catch them safely, one by one? I really hope they can find kind people to take them in soon...
QuestLetter/362:
Hi there, I'm a friend of Miki. 

Lately, Miki's been telling me that a bunch of kittens have been causing quite a commotion in the supermarket. They're getting into everything and seriously affecting business. We're also worried they might get hurt while chasing each other around the furniture.

QuestLetter/1041:
<PlayerName>, your outstanding performance in the town has greatly enhanced the company's reputation—well done. 

According to our research, many residents are looking to settle in the town. After discussions with local real estate developers, we've decided to assign you to provide model room arrangement services.  

We've already designated the first room, and all the furniture you need will be provided by the company. We look forward to seeing your excellent work.
QuestLetter/371:
Hey, it's Jane again! I really loved what you did with the instrument store. 

This time, I want to ask for your help with my sister's new project. She's planning to open a music-themed cocktail bar right here in town—a kind of underground amusement park for young folks. I'll be performing there regularly too. 

Could you help us set up the place? We're going for a punk rock vibe, the kind that hits you with a wave of music the moment you walk in. Please say yes!!
